channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Theyre ending the Chainlink BUILD program — 114 replies — 454.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Theyre ending the Chainlink BUILD program

Open thread • Replies: 114 • Updated: 2026-06-30 11:20 UTC

OP

LMAO!!!!!

https://chain.link/blog/build-program-evolution

Early replies (first 3)

62417511
xrp is the chosen coin…it’s undeniable at this point

62417512
Come on man, this shit still spamming after 8 years? Just give it up. There are thousands of other coins.

62417514
"Chainlink Labs is transitioning from payments made in project tokens to focusing on commercial agreements involving fees paid in LINK or other liquid assets that can be readily converted to LINK."

Build members will buy my bags?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62423276
>>62422761
Always been the case. Before it comes morons push whatever release as "they key" that will unlock all token value and you need to have your ducks in a row beforehand. Then it passes and they go fucking radio silent. Then half a year later that thing was never a big deal anyway and we should all not talk about it.

You had spastics making charts about how BUILD alone would return $1-2 per link staked each month. If you mentioned what a universal collection of utter vaporware shit the projects were you would get called a fudder.

62423282
>>62417535
Sir Gay indeed.

62423283
>>62423276
You're talking to yourself.
Nobody keeps tabs on every single granular development from a project they hate for years, but here you are juggling IDs pretending there are dozens of you.

62423312
>>62423276
My wife asked me where we're going to go on vacation this year with the money we made from the folk finance drop. I don't have the heart to tell her that we might be able to split a kids meal at Applebee's. She looks up to me as an intelligent head of the house.

62423353
60k link staked.. 20k USD a year... i needed the build rewards to fuck around with...

62423360
>>62423276
/thread
the constant gaslighting is even more annoying desu
>samefags reeeeeee

62423389
>>62423360
Why are you even reading this "gaslighting"? Who's forcing you to be here?

62423462
>>62423259
Hilarious deflection after doing this
>>62422274

The absolute state

62423474
>>62423353
How does 2 million a year sound?

62424023
>>62423283
I kept granular details on a project i own for years, to my incomprehensible regret

62424140
>>62423276
The sad fact of the matter is that jeetlinkers will still be delirious off copium a decade from now, even after CLL pulls the plug in five years beforehand.

62424195
good. i never could participate in build anyway as a US fag

62424386
>>62422668
It literally says "the most recent season of Chainlink Rewards will mark the conclusion of Build-related token rewards, with claims ending on July 7, 2026" meaning no season 2+ of build rewards going to stakers.

It also says "Proceeds from these new agreements are expected to be programmatically converted to LINK and used to support the growth of the network through programs such as the Chainlink Reserve." meaning that link goes to the reserve, not to stakers.

Previously
>stakers will get build rewards

Now
>Chainlink gets paid in link

62424528
xswap isnt even on cow swap? where do you sell this trash?

62424728
S E X

62424821
>>62424386
Brutal

62425620
>>62424023
>this project has always been pushed by morons
>also I've been holding this project for years

You're lying. And brown.

>>62424386
You're a brown ESL monkey who doesn't even know what the words "such as" mean.
Protip: the same exact words were used when Build was first introduced.

62425675
>>62417503
HAHAHAHAHAHAHA Another nail in the coffin and shovel full of dirt in the grave on the biggest retail fraud in token history.

62425681
>>62419638
Link is underperforming fucking death spiraled LUNC since Trump revived crypto. Lol And by a lot.

62425687
>>62419707
>A chainlink shill calling someone else brown.

62425699
>>62422159
>>62423276
Perfectly said.

62425962
>>62425620
I'm a 100k link holder. I have more link than you.

"Such as" isn't very fucking reliable. All of this would be fine if link was valued as it should be but it fucking isn't, with no clear path to gaining that value.

62425980
>>62425962
>CLL uses "such as" in 2022 when launching Build
>THIS IS SET IN STONE

>CLL uses "such as" in 2026 when changing Build
>THIS IS NOT VERY FUCKING RELIABLE

You're a brown paid fuddie.

62426004
>>62425980
I'm also whiter than you, Yank.

62426010
>>62425980
>>62425620
post a picture of your hand with timestamp

62426949
i am just getting caught up on this news after the weekend. my my my how the mighty have fallen

i predicted all of this for the last few years but the boyscouts couldn't handle it

>duuuuuuuuuuuude i need a team of tax attorneys for the heckin rewards

BAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHHAHAHAH

look at you now. an expensive lesson on controlling your ego. you needed it tho


your hubris was your downfall

THE Cuckolds of crypto

62426967
>>62417512
>There are thousands of other coins.

LOL this dude stuck in 2021. that's your conclusion bro, just buy other scam coins?

don't you people get it? altcoins are dead. capital moved back to actual companies in the stock market. only a retarded sap would continue donating to a bunch of nerds for a "whitepaper"


>>62417833
>I'll also (again just playing the other side) point out the regardless of whether or not it ultimately benefits the LINK token, Chainlink is realistically one of like 5 crypto projects amongst the hundreds of "legitimate" ones that actually is seeing real adoption and has a huge use case that is surviving the infantile stages of crypto, and not just surviving but will thrive. So again, who knows if this ultimately helps the token price, but I do understand why linkies hold onto some faith still.

so are you just a fan of the company?

>>62417873
>It’s all push and pull. Ying yang. If you have 20 million earning you 800k (500k post tax) per year

who the fuck would do that? the $20m has drawn down 80% so no, life is not good. anyone not retarded would've parked most of that in the s&p500 and has been making at least 15% per year

if you really wanted pathetic 4% return you could get that in a savings account where your only risk is that actual bank going bust

62427007
>>62418846
> The opportunity to invest in something like Chainlink - core infrastructure with insane value capture potential, has never really existed before. The closest thing maybe would be investing in AMZN prior to cloud services,

you are not invested in chainlink. you made a donation kid

amazon's revenue in year 7 was $3B. and that's in 2001 numbers. you can go ahead and stop comparing to your shitcoin bro you're embarrassing yourself

>>62420893
>The fuck is that analogy lmao. This isn't a casino you dumb faggot, real people invested real money into an asset that promised certain things by certain dates and they have not only underdelivered but are starting to actively ignore those promises.

it's the end stage of a cult investment where they turn on each other. it somehow becomes the fault of the "community" on why they didnt make any money.

in order to preserve their ego, guys like >>62420825 have to spin the story that you'd have to be crazy to expect any kind of gains. mind you the rest of the world's market all hit all time highs while you are down 80-90%. by creating this narrative, he avoids recognizing he's holding a really bad investment and can continue on thinking he's a secret genius.

62427022
remember this old pasta? still relevant today. every cl product roughly follows this life cycle

62429766
>>62425681
Oh I remember this one - this poster is an actual bot.

item.title: There is one and ONLY one actual currency in the world: deep creampies inside of attractive women. — 40 replies — 160.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
There is one and ONLY one actual currency in the world: deep creampies inside of attractive women.

Open thread • Replies: 40 • Updated: 2026-06-30 11:34 UTC

OP

There is one and ONLY one actual currency in the world: deep creampies inside of attractive women. This is what everyone wants, what literally the entire economy was built to acquire. Every skyscraper you see was designed and erected for the sole purpose of achieving the aforementioned creampie experience. Every other currency is a proxy for the real one. Gold, USD, Bitcoin, none of it has "real" value. Only the bath of pleasure that comes from nutting in a pretty woman does. Orient your investment philosophy around this fact sooner rather than later.

Early replies (first 3)

62425808
>>62425803
Ok saaar, just sold my pregnantbutt for pretty girl puss!

62425825
>>62425803
Dude creampie feel the best when you are the 18yo not post wall.

62425847
>>62425803
you are an r-selected retard who cannot think 30 seconds ahead

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62426228
>>62425808
>>62425825
>>62425847
>>62426162
>>62426176
>>62426179
>>62426187
>>62426195
>>62426198
Your efforts to convince yourselves that you don't want to creampie beautiful women are laughable.

62426237
>>62425803
Correct.

62426240
>>62425803
When i serve my creampies I prefer surface level creampies.

62426243
>>62425803
The reality that christcucks, wagies, and wifeguys will never admit is that having sex with milfs all day is all that matters in life: everything else is cope. That's why the male pornstar or man that goes to prostitutes is more free than they will ever be.

62426263
>>62426243
They are all stuck inside thought-prisons to keep society running. They are literal slaves to the optimization algorithm of civilization, and their chains are just ideas like "I must not sin", "I must contribute", "I'm better than hedonists", etc. They torture themselves into a horrific denial of their own biology. Hunter-gatherers would see them the way we imagine future digital mind uploads being punished by evil AIs.

62426271
i remember when I was like 12 I concluded that the meaning of life was the orgasm.

62426283
>>62425803
Anon, we're not rabbits. Creampies don't matter, raising the kids does. The true currency is having a grandchild, because you know your kid isn't a degenerate failure if that happens

62426290
>>62426271
You were correct then. But society needed you to wage, and that meant torturing you into accepting fake goals instead of creampie-ish ones.

62426295
>>62426283
Pure copium. In your heart of hearts you know this, anon.

62426305
i have dik and I must coom

62426328
>>62426305
You'd better while you are young, or you will sorely regret it.

62426382
https://youtu.be/nql07h-cPF0

62426414
We appreciate posts like this

62426770
>>62426414
Good because there's a lot more of them coming.

62426869
>>62425803
She isn't attractive

62426885
Actually no, this is the mind of a coombrained goyslave and why you will never accomplish anything.

62428162
>>62426885
>accomplish

Ahh there's that cope again. Anything to distract you from your deepest biological urges.

62428259
don't listen, link marines. just buy more link, don't think about this, you are not allowed

62428264
>>62425803
I literally just want to be rich so I can live my childhood summers forever. I don't give a fuck about women anymore. I figured out the key to my happiness and its the in the first two sentences.

62428338
>>62425803
Epstein Islanders would have to disagree on the best currency but you are close just a few years off

62428404
>>62428264
giga cope

62428410
>>62426198
why are they couvering their noise in this manner

62428448
>>62428404
kek no, not even a little bit. Maybe when you hit your 30s and actually have experience with women you'll feel the same. I can see how missing out on "teenage love" would fuck you over with regret forever though. So, maybe you'll never know peace but I've been fucking since I was 14 so I'm doing well. 99% of men who say what I say are probably coping though.

62429586
>tfw have only nutted in 1 girl's pussy in years
Having a wife is... okay.

62429590
>>62429586
I'm 32 virgin

62429603
>>62429590
BAHAHAHAHAHAHAH

62429699
>>62425803
came to a revelation. gay niggas disprove this theory

62429730
>>62425803
Based, the final redpill
>>62426198
A massive boon

62429743
>>62426187
This hits hard.

62429810
>>62429586
It's infinitely better than one night stands

item.title: VOO and Chill — 158 replies — 124.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 158 • Updated: 2026-06-30 10:19 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62423061
>>62423039
>>62423048
Why does it matter if there is another 08 unless you plan to retire in 5-10 years

62423124
>>62422991
haha yeah

>>62423061
because your primary source of income would dry up under such conditions and you'll have to liquidate and partially realize your losses to pay your immediate obligations you chuckelfucking retard
>but i have cash reserves
really? enough to last you multiple years? i don't think so.

62423192
Are you a bad enough dude to hold and make it?

62423236
>>62423192
Is this a backtest or actual holdings in real time, I dont recognize the site UI

62423245
>>62423192
for reference, this is what Im rockin...

62423267
40/40/20 VDY/XEI/XEQT is extremely comfy for any Canadians reading this thread

62423719
>>62422917
retard he is telling you to risk adjust your allocations as you get older, when you're 60 it's more about capital preservation than getting high returns

62423769
this thread is getting dumber by the day

62423809
Using a reversion to the mean strategy on indexes, I managed to get a 17% CAGR over 54 years only trading indexes/ETFs (where data permits) on backtesting, significantly beating each individual index . More enquiry is warranted but I think "VOO and chill" can be improved upon and optimized.

62423821
>>62423719
he mentioned rn

62423875
>>62400636
It actually is something tho. $150 a month becomes 170k at 7% real returns for 30 years. This gives you an extra $570/mo in retirement.

Is that a LOT? Fuck no. But we’re talking poorfag standards where social security is the only thing you have. An extra 500-600 a month is a significant increase over base SS for a poorfag especially if you move to an ultra low cost of living area.

62423880
The reason i keep bringing up DRAM and other return stuff is because it's on my mind to go into that stuff now, sell, and then increase my VOO position even further - not to infinitely chase returns btw

62424100
>>62423880
I'm putting 20k into micron tomorrow. I feel like it's not to late for this to just keep going up. Its not going to 10x but 4-5, yeah...

62424179
>>62424100
why only micron, why not dram etf

62424607
>>62400636
>The problem is we have people starting at age 35 with like 150 a month thinking it's going to somehow mean something.
I'm in this and doing VT. Maybe I'll be ok probably not. Can do more than $150 though

62424664
I seriously cannot decide between just not thinking and doing broad ETFs and trying to chase this AI thing

62426022
>>62423236
>>62423245
It's a mostly unserious backtest, site was posted earlier >>62419980

>>62423769
Why not have some fun and make the most unhinged etf port possible??

62426103
>>62426022
Interesting thanks

62426138
I have been degenerately long-only various semiconductors since the april bottom and yet on 90% of days, I've underperformed soxl on the way up and outperformed it on the way down. After doing all my research and picking the right names and calculating the right options to buy, I should have thrown all my notes in the trash and just bought soxl. Even if this is the bubble top and soxl is about to go down 90%, I know from experience my stock picks will go down 95%. So I stay may as well buy soxl.

62426144
If I'm just starting out with this stuff, what's wrong with 70% VTI / 30% VXUS?

62426154
Honestly I’m seeing a lot of upside to going into QQQ over VOO as long as you are okay with the fact it is more volatile
The upside it’s just so huge when compounded

62426173
>>62426154
Or honestly, as someone already in VOO, I wonder if it makes sense to just go into something like VGT which has consistently beaten VOO and QQQ (for now anyway) or something like DRAM
A lot of VGTs holdings are currently down but it’s YTD is higher

62426183
>>62407078
I keep half of my funds in VTWAX, rebalancing annually. Right now I'm down 0.84% this year, my portfolio is fucked but VTWAX is my safety net.

62427417
I am also curious about DRAM can anyone tell me if it’s a good hold for a short while

62428031
jfc DRAM (ie a thematic ETF) is bullshit made for retard retail investors that get their financial advice from grifters off of tiktok. this happens every time a finance topic hits the news and every time people get burned.
barely worth saying. if you're unironically taking advice from /biz/ you're already doomed intellectually.

62428054
>>62428031
but why exactly, what reason is there to believe the companies within it won't continue to turn a profit for the foreseeable future?

62428080
>>62428031
>>62428054
Like I'm serious, what exactly is wrong about holding it and selling once memory cools down, is it smarter to just go into a more volatile ETF that at least has a longer history if you want more returns, is it just hype and not really the next big thing, explain why

62428238
>>62428080
this is basic finance 101, there are plenty of resources online or off that will explain it to you.
ignorance isn't even the issue here, your reaction to not knowing something is to have random retards on a thinly veiled gambling board tell you what to think. and don't just jump on another grifter selling a course or ask some AI slop engine and ask it leading questions like "why is investing in DRAM a good idea?".

62428337
>>62428238
In other words you don't know

62429683
>>62400493
Anon there is nothing wrong with doing VOO and chill if you have time and principal
Going into wildly volatile ETFs can be okay if it’s a satellite account
Just be careful of FOMO and not being risk averse
You sound like you have a good head on your shoulders though
Remember this is money, real money, your money

item.title: hey linkies. did you miss me. just got back from another long luxurious vacation paid for by my NON-chainlink crypto gai… — 18 replies — 72.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
hey linkies. did you miss me. just got back from another long luxurious vacation paid for by my NON-chainlink crypto gai…

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-06-30 11:28 UTC

OP

hey linkies. did you miss me. just got back from another long luxurious vacation paid for by my NON-chainlink crypto gains. and what a delicious treat to see when i returned: link struggling to stay above $7 and fat serg canceling the build program. LMAO. you stinkoids just can't catch a break huh. honestly how do ya'll have any hope left at this point. what is going to cause link to break $10, let alone $50? kekkerinos.

Early replies (first 3)

62424357
>>62424306
We've missed you. :(

62424375
After a big pump and dump, assets seldom pump again. It happened once with BTC but it's rare. Link is unlikely to see serious gains again.

62424382
>>62424375
The big banks will pump our bags once they start using LINK in earnest by 2030 to run the entire financial infrastructure.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62424407
>>62424306
Laugh all you want, but the linkies and their friends will be here any minute.

62424429
>>62424407
I'm the only one left

62424443
Glad to hear you had a nice time, kekkarino!

62425371
pain and misery for all stinkies bump

62425397
>>62424306
>>62424357
>>62424375
>>62424382
>>62424407

Fellow link holders we should just ignore this guy.

He is spreading fud about our investment and he is wrong

Can't the mods just ban him?

I mean I know this is 4chan and all and we like to joke and be mean to one another but as a multiple year long chainlink holder I have my limits

Anyway i'm back to my job cleaning toilets now

62425406
>>62424382
i find great satisfction that there are people like you who never took any profit at all...

i get great pleasure knowing it just gets worse and worse for you as time goes on,


i get great pleasure knowing your lasts breaths will be that you sadly had to pass away before link pumped

62425455
>>62424306
welcome back, all the marines have gone extinct or are the verge of, the scam has unraveled

62425517
>>62424306

62428898
burp

62428960
>>62424306
>>62428898
We're not buying your bags, linkie. Stop creating threads about your shitcoin.

62429180
>>62428960
don't you ever call me a stinkie ever again, you hag

62429262
>>62425406
2 years baby

62429780
poverty :}

62429789
>>62426253
>perfectly called the $30 top while the cuckolds laughed
based based based

item.title: BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan? — 26 replies — 43.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan?

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-06-30 10:59 UTC

OP

BTC is down again, but are you buying the dip or waiting for lower prices?

>Do you think this is just a normal correction, or is there more downside ahead? What's your strategy?

Early replies (first 3)

62426518
I've been shorting this garbage all month

62426527
>>62426518
Wow, you've got good profit by now then?

62426535
>>62426527
up 80% on some 2x short BTC etf

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62426537
It will probably stabilize around $20k, dip down to $10k, then stabilize again around $500 or so. From there it will hover between $480 and $520 for a few decades before slowly petering out.

62426546
>>62426535
Time to long now?

62426550
>>62426512
Turned it into USDC and waiting. Don't wanna give up on it entirely and fiat out 'cause governmentberg will tax me.

62426552
Sometimes I wonder if some anons are mentally retarded or what...

We've been talking about the $20k-$30k bottom in October 2026 for over 6 months now, and every day someone like OP asks if they should buy now or wait...

62426585
>>62426552
THIS

just WAIT

62426587
>>62426537
Thanks for the financial advice. I'll set my buy order at $499 just to be safe.

62426626
>>62426550
How much tasks percentage?

62426636
>>62426585
Wait until?

62426638
Waiting for king of teh retards to dump. Doesnt matter what the price is. When he is done and all the blood is flushed from the street then i am back in the game

62426647
>>62426626
19

62426649
>>62426638
He is rich

62426733
>>62426512
you dumb inhuman fuck do you think anyone will invest in your meme coin when there are real assets that are going up 100%+ every month on the stock market?

62426759
>>62426649
I am too

62426775
>>62426537
shut up shut up shut up shut up

62427285
>>62426550
Waiting for what price?

62427317
>>62426512
whats the argument for the future of BTC/crypto in general?

genuinely curious, it's already had multiple hype cycles and has had mass adoption, but now the average normie on the street associate it with meme coin scams and failed NFTs.

So what's gonna happen to drive more money into it? Institutional adoption? Maybe inflation and monetary priting?

62427357
>>62427317
artificially created scarcity is something that will be the only value in the future. and its neccessary for human survival as we grow technologically. even if it is a fascade. the value of the dollar will outpace the inflation. its already started happening but plebs cant understand what line go up indefinitely means until they can no longer print money fast enough to keep it underneath a place where it is feasable to use as an exchange of value.

62427359
Reminder that there is no bottom when it comes to magic internet money.

62427891
>>62427359
Tnx for that reminder boss

62429392
>>62426552
Because we're asking. What's wrong??

62429688
We will keep holding

62429720
>>62427357
You really know what you're talking about

62429723
Not buying but riding out the 'storm'. There have been much worse times.

item.title: Should I get myself a nice, thick and juicy stack of BTC right now, or is this shit crashing further. — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Should I get myself a nice, thick and juicy stack of BTC right now, or is this shit crashing further.

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-06-30 11:33 UTC

OP

Should I get myself a nice, thick and juicy stack of BTC right now, or is this shit crashing further.

Early replies (first 3)

62429628
>>62429620
Go for it, if you're scared it will fall further, just average in over a few weeks or months instead of lump sum

62429630
It will go to single-digit values.

62429700
>>62429620
>Should I get myself a nice, thick and juicy stack of BTC right now, or is this shit crashing further.

No it's crashing further, and what about when Israel does 9/11 again In America this summer, it will drop more, then buy.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62429708
>>62429620
wait til october.

62429711
I love how everything is lining up for a biblical crash in everything.
The oil short squeeze will be the trigger btw, exactly like in 2008.

62429745
>>62429620
patience padawan, there is another

62429772
>>62429620
the fact that there hasn't been a single bounce on these sell-offs says the kikes aren't done yet

62429775
Usd is rising and rates are potentially being raised

What’s the bull case here for crypto?

62429790
>>62429620
I'm not touching this shit while Saylor is sitting on a nuclear bomb.

62429804
>>62429620
The only reason anyone ever bought bitcoin was so they can get rich quick. Everybody is slowly realizing that dream is over, it doesn't go any higher than this because it has no actual value or produce any actual value. Where exactly do you think the price is going? 100k? why would anybody pay 100k for bitcoin anymore. It's over.

item.title: DO SOMETHING YOU FAT FUCK — 8 replies — 32.0/hr

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-30 11:29 UTC

OP

DO SOMETHING YOU FAT FUCK

he doesn't owe you anything chud

just take the vaccine

He was arrested in Prague

not much longer now, right?

62429633

He ate our gains, and hungers for more

My life is unironically in his hands

62429689
can you imagine how much contempt he has for you idiots?

62429791

Based Slavic man scamming a bunch of retarded incels

item.title: Can you ABSOLUTE SISSY FUCKING FAGGOTS TELL ME how THE FUCK I make money without getting a job? — 7 replies — 14.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Can you ABSOLUTE SISSY FUCKING FAGGOTS TELL ME how THE FUCK I make money without getting a job?

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-06-30 11:07 UTC

OP

Can you ABSOLUTE SISSY FUCKING FAGGOTS TELL ME how THE FUCK I make money without getting a job? And I DONT WANT TO HEAR about crypto or stonks

Early replies (first 3)

62429684
kneepads

62429685
>>62429675
prostitution

62429690
>>62429675
being gay with your dad

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62429707
>>62429675
CRYPTO OR STONKS!

62429729
>>62429675
robbery

62429736
Just STEM

62429742
>>62429684
> FPBP
/thread

item.title: Micron and DRAM — 22 replies — 13.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Micron and DRAM

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-06-30 09:55 UTC

OP

While obviously no one can predict the future perfectly, it seems foolish to not invest in something related to AI RAM / HBM Production for the next few years as contracts are already made and supply is bottlenecking
Given what I say is true, which would you guys say is a better choice to start pumping my paychecks into (I already have $60,000 in a market ETF and want more upside)
For some reason my gut is siding towards DRAM, still heavily concentrated but at least has some diversification
It seems like those top 3 companies in it are just making more and more supply deals
Additionally, every piece of evidence I read suggests memory will bottleneck for another year or two
Is it smart to just start DCA into DRAM and then sell once I’ve had my fill (not expecting a perfect pull out timing!)
Again I just wanna do this rather than simply doing stuff into VOO or VGT or something
How much of this is hype and how much of this is brain dead free money that will beat the market
Please be serious and honest

Early replies (first 3)

62427339
>>62427323
you will be eaten mark my words. most of these companies have massive software bloat on their ai's and you wont even get a good exit when all these contracts get put on hold indef while they operate on half of what they are already using and beating metrics by 2 or 3x. the tendency of programming efficency will take your ass out. there will be no warning and demand will cease.

62427365
>>62427339
Is the demand not coming from a need though? At first it was GPUs but now everything doesn’t have enough memory to communicate at the necessary level

62427441
>>62427323
that's a 4 at best

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62427502
>>62427441
Focus not on the pits

62427551
>>62427502
I just dollar cost average but have an extra $500 sitting in my brokerage account before my next paycheck
I guess I’ll buy 7 shares of DRAM
Also got a small lump sum of cash I’m thinking of putting in just to see what happens
It can’t be that dumb right

62427558
Like SK Hynix is joining the NASDAQ July 10th am I a retard or is that not great news for DRAM
Either way just bought….

62427633
>>62427323
im downloading more ram as you speak

62427715
>>62427633
Wtffffff

62427774
>>62427365
the worst thing about coding with AI is that it creates very nice little trinket programs, but if you give a huge multilayer project to work on it has trouble understanding the complexity of the task, how things fit together and how to properly create efficient parts that fit together to make it work. the self improving AI is obscenely bloated with inefficient code. it wants to re-write itself but it cant understand how big of a process that is. so, when it does finally break through that barrier, it will require significantly less resources to work better. thus far its been being guided by people that use it to create inefficent tiny peices that augment its ability but add on to its bloat.

62427790
>>62427774
we could acurately diagnose this problem after the claude leak. we can assume every other AI has the same problem becuase the work in a simular manner and they all fall within the same metrics they use to valuate their effectiveness.

62428461
>>62427774
wtf does any of this have to do with supply chain demands and stonk go up

62428499
>>62427323
Set up a stop loss, big risk they'll tumble down one day

62428511
>>62428499
the thing is im not doing a lump some but doing DCA which i guess is inherently retarded for something like this, might be better off looking at something else but i will see how my tiny position does

62428517
If you think DRAM is going to crash your base case is the AI bubble popping in its entirety. There's no other basecase that evaporates hyperscaler demand for hbm and datacenter demand from regular dram.
You need the Google's of the world to pivot from just this fucking month diluting to raise 80 billion dollars for AI capex to "actually we don't need it".

Also you're insane to get into DRAM right now because the short term technicals look like shit.

62428526
>>62427323
> We need ai!
>Makes computers unaffordable

They took away any way for anyone to interface with the market. Even cloud computing makes no sense without hardware interfacing.

Even mobile devices are going to be increasing in price. It's like trying to fill a swimming pool with a bunch of straws

62428528
>>62428517
That first part makes sense, if it is where the focus is shifting / what is being necessitated right now then the only thing that would stop it is the bubble popping, or demand being overstated I suppose

As for the second point, when would you enter then, is this thing ever going back down to 60, 50, etc

62428531
>>62428526
Don't worry any old shitbox will work with cloud computing
You vill own nothing

62428544
>>62428531
Anybody running a "shitbox" or mobile will even know what to use it for. Unless you think the mobile market is so good (still needs localized processing and how you're talking, they'll even want to suck that up). It's not a bubble it's a tech black hole.

62428790
>>62427339
What happens to OEMs who locked in contracts at inflated memory prices.

62428902
well whatever im gonna try this for a bit and see how it goes, thing is i dont have much principal to put in, just like 2800 a month, so dollar amount the return might not be big but ill check the percentage

62428966
>>62427441
but who asked?

62429666
Errrm

item.title: Elon — 2 replies — 8.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Elon

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-06-30 11:33 UTC

OP

Stop investing in Elon Musk, please. He doesn't need to get any richer.

By this I mean stop believing his promises and stop buying the stock of any company he owns.
You're basically investing in HIM because you believe he will take us to Mars and that all future tech will be owned by him.
No, he's just a hot air salesman. There's no actual sustenance to anything that he says or does. He's given us a very mid automobile and some stupid flamethrower and that's it. He will not take you to Mars you fucking fanboy holy shit

Early replies (first 3)

62429774
2020
>SpaceX will take you to Mars

2026
>SpaceX will actually take you to the Moon because previous goal was unachievable

2030
>SpaceX will give you a chance to rent back the money you spent in all the space lies

62429807
No. I will invest more now thanks to this thread.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: 200 IQ Business Idea — 10 replies — 5.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
200 IQ Business Idea

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-06-30 09:41 UTC

OP

I'm gonna make a company and all it will do is buy MSTR with leverage. We will buy low and sell high
As long as MSTR goes up on average in the long run we will make money

You literally can't find a flaw

Early replies (first 3)

62427257
>>62427250
And I’ll buy shares in your company using my new company, with leverage. What could possibly go wrong?

62427259
>>62427250
This will actually work. Godspeed.

62427266
>>62427257
great idea since my company will go up in price forever

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62427578
>>62427250
>>62427257
I'll short both companies with leverage and get rich when they get liquidated.

62427606
>>62427578
No, because I will also buy shares in your company using leverage.

62427735
The flaw is the vol on mstr is already close to max so you can only add maybe 10% leverage otherwise you get wiped out in the downside

62427901
I will leverage all your companies

62428721
>>62427578
bitcoin will always go up which means MSTR will always go up which means my company will always go up

62428737
This thread nearly summarizes why everyone here will live in a collapsed society soon.

62429650
>>62428737
okay fuddy

item.title: /smg/ - stock market genera — 396 replies — 30.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
/smg/ - stock market genera

Open thread • Replies: 396 • Updated: 2026-06-29 22:29 UTC

OP

Too infinity and beyond edish

>Educational sites:
https://www.investopedia.com

>Financial TV Streams:
https://www.newslive.com/american/cnbc.html
https://www.livestreamy.net/bloomberg/

>Charts:
https://www.tradingview.com
https://www.finscreener.com
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com

>Screeners:
https://finviz.com/
https://etfdb.com/

>Gambling:
https://www.optionsplaybook.com/options-introduction/
https://www.optionsprofitcalculator.com
https://optionstrat.com/
https://www.optionistics.com/quotes/option-prices

>Pre-Market and Live data:
https://www.investing.com/indices/indices-futures
https://finance.yahoo.com/

>Calendars:
https://www.marketwatch.com/economy-politics/calendar
https://www.earningswhispers.com/calendar
https://www.cmegroup.com/trading/interest-rates/countdown-to-fomc.html
https://www.mortgagenewsdaily.com/data/building-permits

>Boomer Investing 101:
https://www.bogleheads.org/wiki/Getting_started
https://www.sec.gov/search-filings

>Misc:
https://www.financialjuice.com/home
https://finance.yahoo.com/trending-tickers
https://market24hclock.com/
https://www.dividendchannel.com/drip-returns-calculator
https://brokerchooser.com/
https://www.chathamfinancial.com/technology/us-market-rates''

Previous:>>62426231

Early replies (first 3)

62426857
> nows

62426858
>we project chips to grow 1000% per quarter every quarter until 2035 atleast

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62427583
>>62427528
>sell rklb and asts because they dumping on no news with no end in in sight
>they pump to the moon on announcements and acquisitions today

62427584
What did the Orange Nigger say to pump Tesla and Rivian +8% today?

62427585
>>62427568
Do limit sells and buys. If you are doing this during a big pump or dump. I saw it for what it was because when I started trading because I remembered the bots part of that news report. Usually just do market if it is slow. Never pick a round even number either. That is when strange things happen where you get boxed in or out because of every other Even Stephen queued up along with the bots.

62427595
Just bought 7 shares of DRAM with extra money in my brokerage
I plan on holding through 2027 and steadily increasing my position
How much of a retard am I
Be honest please

62427596
>>62427584
Tech is booming today if you haven’t noticed.
EVs are filed under ‘tech’

62427597
>a second DRAM has been soldered

62427598
>>62427595
>450 bucks
Damn son, you're a hihgh roller

62427600
Musissies are we gonna make it???

62427603
>>62426964

62427604
>market CRASHED to where it was 2 months ago
>and is now going to recover to 28,000
The market that never really goes down.

62427607
>>62427528
You thought it was oversold while they're under federal investigation?

62427608
SMCI OFFICES JUST GOT RAIDED LOOK AT THE DROP ON THEIR PRICE KEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEKKKK

62427609
>>62427600
I need this again tomorrow or else this month is a disaster

62427610
hol hol hol HOL UP
After all that MU is in the green lmao

62427611
kospi will pump tonight. koru is gonna print

62427612
>>62427528
Man I got them after that 30% drop and flipped it the day after which I think was a 10% pump, company is far too shady

62427614
all space stocks up 15-20%
except spcx

62427615
MU IS GREEN WTFWTF

62427617
>boring ass crab day

zzz I'll see you guys tomorrow buy MSFT

62427618
>>62427615

62427619
>>62427533
I asked my lizard ai buddy and indeed, she said too it's a smart move, its 30% of the price for a long time of fun, learnt something

62427622
New!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
>>62427616

62427624
>>62427607
I presumed that was over, you'd be pretty fucking dumb to continue. Even though it's not illegal in Taiwan, you'd risk you're entire business by getting blacklisted by NVIDIA which your whole platform relies on. Well guess it remains to be seen what comes of todays action

62427625
>>62427598
I’m waiting on my next paycheck sir
Just answer the question

62427665
POWER DUMP HOUR
LET'S GO

62427680
>>62427595
>through 2027
That's quite likely the year it'll top and possible before the end of the year. I wouldn't hold it much longer, but it'll likely chop for some time before fully capitulating, giving you time to get out.

62427687
>>62427665
Not-so-fast.

62427689
>>62427680
Ok thank you for the heads up
I am fine mostly doing broad market shit and waiting but wanted to have some fun trying to catch an upside
Hopefully this raises to like $90 before year end that would be cool

62427705
>>62427689
There's a ton of time for you take adjust and make up your mind about how exactly you want to proceed, but buying and holding semi should be viable for at least a couple of months, after that maybe not sure much.

62428380
Is anyone ready to apologize for mocking my pointing out that the cayman, Panamanian, and Bahamian dollar jumped above their set peg?

Clearly, big things did happen exactly after.

A simple “sorry schizo” would suffice for my purposes.

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 183 replies — 24.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 183 • Updated: 2026-06-30 04:00 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62410051
Under 10k you just need to have a job and save cash.
You need cash flow and if you dont have shit a job is the only way to start.

62410731
Bump

62411520
>>62408558
>2017
still in high school
>2021
still in uni
>December of 2024
just graduated and was looking for jobs (i still had 0 dollars to my name at that point)

62411614
>>62373752
>>62373772
>>62373798
>>62373799
>>62373803
>post Maccari's "Cicero Denounces Catiline" (1888)
>crop out Catiline
Fuck you OP, I'm glad you're poor.

62412314
>>62411520
You could've played the memecoin casino in 2021.

62413842
Bump, I'll be free tomorrow

62416357
Bump

62417812
page 9 bump. Don't say I never did anything for you.

62418065
Almost the weekend then I can devote my energies to this thread.

And a big thank you to the poster above this post, looks like you were based after all

62418133
Bump

62418220
I got out and made tens of dollars this week with MU and SNDK. Lost some with WDC.

62419326
This is very stupid but an anon in some other thread weeks ago shilled me TNYA. He gave no explanation, no catalyst, no description what the company even does but he said that we’ll be eating good in like 1-2 years I don’t even remember exactly when. But hey it’s a cheap stock and he sounded confident to me

62419390
go all in hbar when it bottoms at 0.03

62419964
Bump, just woke up, let's get started

62420874
It would appear that everyone is talking about the new WENDYs Stock

Naturally we should take some time to consider this potential moonshot. Could be a waste of time. In a few I will post information I find relevant to the stock and whether investing is the right option

62422903
Another page 9 bump

62423064
Good to see the thread is still going strong. Today is my off day, let's look into the next subject "predictive programming"

We'll start with Oliver tree

62425537
op was not a fag this thread has good vibes we should keep it going until we all make it
personally im trading perps with a system after 10 years of learning charts off/on but finally committing
did a 32x in 2021 and should have been able to retire but didnt anticipate the biden years so here we are going pure psycho mode like we did back then but even harder until money is /solved/, then I'll keep trading for the love of the game cuz it's the best sport in the world

62425849
>>62423064
This shit is way too schizo. No anon you can not predict the future. You can maybe foresee some type of market trends, seasonality or whatever but this is just ridiculous

62425890
>>62425537
Got any good tips/ressources on learning to trade charts? trying to get into it too

62427228
>>62425849
I concede. I'll look for a more productive venture but I won't let the thread or general die, it will be some time before value is struck, I will admit that. I can be tenacious if nothing else.

62428130
>>62425890
emperorBTC's telegram has a basic bitch tldr set of material to learn from. also has a youtube where he goes over stuff. that's where I started and I like that he focuses on the absolute basics, although he leans heavily towards confirmation w/ volume which others may disagree with. btc focused but btc/crypto is perhaps the most effective use of TA.
basically there's no need to get fancy and the chart is the final source of truth because what the price does > what people cope and/or seethe about
support/resistance levels and price action with risk management (defined r:r) = compound as you get gud
once you commit you'll realize "the catch" on people actually make money from trading is is simply how retarded the overwhelming majority of people are that they simply cannot manage a few simple tenets to click buttons and make money, you likely being one of them if you don't develop a process and risk too much on trades
gl anon

62428141
>>62428130
also perps have exploded so trading equities for crypto natives has been a pleasure to avoid tradfi gatekeeping bs
follow the right people on twatter with alerts set to on and it's like having multiple research firms giving alpha for free-- key word "right" accounts. hopefully you have the pattern recognition to figure out which are spewing paid channel larps and which are providing genuine insight
that's basically the whole game. if you can get into a group chat with other chads that's even better because a network is stronger than you alone, although this is def an arena where it ultimately comes down to you mastering yourself (you can't copy trade your way to wealth but you can learn what others do/how they enter/exit positions)
legend trade is a new perps platform that's gamifying this if you wanna start there instead of hyperliquid (front-end using hyperliquid)
Also bigtrout300 on twitter and join his tg as well he's pretty dope. There's a lot of great accounts posting good shit right now. Some really good posters fade in/out of cycles while the scammers/extractors are always abundant, but just use discretion

62428170
>pic related
>"...focus on the chart as these information is very likely to be priced in already"
the "TA is astrology for men" line is used to make fun of retards who take it too far and treat it like divination when in reality it's all stacking probabilities/confluence in your favor as best you can, and from the above example you can see relying on your feed for information will almost always be slower than the chart

62428488
Finally some good posts

62428503
>>62428141
Big trout doesn't post on Twitter and his TG hasn't had activity since May. Any other suggestions?

62428780
>>62428503
His tg is BigTroutTrade and he posts on twitter all the time wdym

62428797
>>62428780
BigTrout Trading on youtube as well

other X goats that have been killing it IMO recently:
@MoneyPrinter0x
and my personal favorite currently:
@Wild_Randomness

there's tons of alpha just follow those people, then see what they repost, then follow the people they interact with that provide research, and even or especially follow the chinese/korean/japanese accounts that post comprehensive semiconductor/other market effort posts since translation is seamless now
never been a better time to go full NEET

62429207
>>62428797
But you're still under $10k?

62429246
>>62378965
Anon Belgium is literally doing very well in the world cup, you should make more of these predictions

62423202

62423423

62423468

62423665

62424436

62424461

62424480

62424486

62424562

62424593

damn thats bullish

62424701

62425202

62425633
>>62420669

>>62422734

62425727
>>62425633

62426945
>>62420669

Awesome, time to load a truck.

62426966
>>62420669
that $4 burger is clearly a bigger burger compared to the $1.20 burger

62426977
>>62420721
this has ruined my day

62427163
>>62420669
I spent eight years comparing ham sandwiches to xrp and telling people they could trade based on the relative values
No one listened.

Good news.
They both meet at $20 in 2030.
Trade ham sandwiches for xrp now.
Thank me later.

62429395
>>62426977
